title: A conservative update with constant flux leaves the state unchanged

source.notes: Combines the conservative update definition with the zero flux-difference structure of a constant flux.

informal.statement: For any update scale, if the flux is constant across all grid indices, then the conservative update equals the original state.
assumptions: scale and c are real numbers
u is a real-valued grid state
i is a natural-number grid index
the flux is the constant function with value c
informal.expected_conclusion: The conservative update with constant flux equals u_i.

declarations.name: conservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: def conservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := u i - scale * fluxDifference flux i

declarations.name: conservativeUpdate_const_flux
declarations.statement: theorem conservativeUpdate_const_flux (scale c : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : conservativeUpdate scale u (fun _ => c) i = u i

tasks.input: Translate the constant-flux conservative update identity into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem conservativeUpdate_const_flux (scale c : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : conservativeUpdate scale u (fun _ => c) i = u i

tasks.input: Given fluxDifference and conservativeUpdate, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding conservativeUpdate and fluxDifference, then closing with ring.
